figures.caption: Table 6: π0.5 30ep per-task closed-loop success rate (%). Each cell = 20 trials. Bold = with-Qwen strictly greater than no-Qwen.
Table 5: OpenVLA-7B 30ep per-task closed-loop success rate (%). Each cell = 20 trials. Bold = α-cell strictly greater than same-row no-Qwen.

Figure 3: We compare phase vocabularies from 3 to 13 categories, averaged over six teacher–suite rollouts. Each panel shows the mean per-phase frame share with standard-deviation error bars across rollouts, reordered so that the largest bin is centered. Coarser taxonomies merge major motion stages into a single dominant bin, whereas finer taxonomies introduce sparse or nearly empty operating sub-categories.

Figure 1: VLA-AD: Overall VLM-Assisted Distillation pipeline. A lightweight Student VLA is trained via parallel offline supervision from a frozen teacher VLA (e.g., OpenVLA-7B or π0.5-4B) and a Vision-Language Model (VLM).

Figure 4: Spurious gripper-command oscillation in OpenVLA-7B teacher rollouts on libero_object task 0. Top: Per-frame gripper command for episode 15; red markers denote sign reversals that revert within ≤ 2 frames. The blue line shows the teacher’s noisy output, while the green line shows the student’s smoothed and robust predictions. Bottom: Five consecutive frames around the flip marked by the dashed line.

Figure 2: Qualitative examples of Qwen2.5-VL phase-anchored descriptions on three OpenVLA-7B rollouts from libero_object. Each panel shows a single frame together with the description Qwen produces under phase-anchored prompt for the phases approaching, transporting, and placing. Highlighted text marks the action-semantic vocabulary that is tied to the phase label.
